landing.heroSub: An AI coach explains <0>every decision in real time</0> — equity, pot odds, opponent range. Animated Range Vision, simulation that <1>proves</1> your edge, cash & tournament training. Become the player others fear.
landing.ctaNote: No credit card · free to start
landing.hook1Desc: On every hand, the coach explains the +EV move: equity, pot odds, opponent range. You understand why — and you improve fast.
landing.hook2Desc: Watch the opponent's range tighten action by action. An animated range read you won't find anywhere else.
landing.hook3Desc: The simulation bench runs thousands of tournaments to PROVE, with hard numbers, that the coach's decisions win.
landing.featuresSub: One tool, from beginner to grinder.
landing.fCash: Play cash with a live coach.
landing.fSim: Measure EV over thousands of hands.
landing.pricingTitle: Start free. Go Pro whenever you want.
landing.finalSub: Join the community and let the coach level you up.
landing.footerDisclaimer: Poker training tool — no real-money gambling, no monetary winnings.

sim.title: Simulation — the coach's test bench
sim.desc: The coach plays tournaments with exactly the same decisions as the autopilot, against bots. We compare it to an average player (a bot in its place) to measure its edge — places, ITM, ROI.
sim.legendMtt: The more mass leans LEFT (high places), the better.
sim.verdictMid: the coach runs a {{r}}% ROI vs {{base}}% for an average player in its place →
sim.verdictBeat: it clearly beats the field by {{pts}} ROI points.
sim.verdictNoEdge: no measurable edge on this sample.
sim.calibNote: (Places CALIBRATED on survival: the average player comes out at ~0% ROI / {{n}}% ITM — so the coach's ABSOLUTE numbers are reliable, not just the gap.)

spotread.title: Spot reading
spotread.intro: Diagnose each hand like a pro: texture → range → equity → bucket. 4 questions per spot.

spotq.textureSemi: Semi-wet — 2 of a suit or one connector
spotq.bucketValue: VALUE — I beat their calling range
spotq.bucketBluffcatch: BLUFF-CATCH — I beat their bluffs, not their value
spotq.bucketDraw: DRAW — behind, but I can hit
spotq.promptTexture: How would you describe the texture of the flop {{board}}?
spotq.promptRange: {{story}} On this flop {{board}}, the range advantage goes to whom?
spotq.promptEquity_one: Against {{count}} opponent on {{board}}, your approximate equity?
spotq.promptEquity_other: Against {{count}} opponents on {{board}}, your approximate equity?
spotq.promptBucket: Your hand {{key}} on {{board}} — which bucket are you in?
spotq.explainTextureDry: Rainbow and disconnected board: few possible draws → ranges don't "connect" much, equity is mostly in made pairs.
spotq.explainTextureWet: Two of a suit AND connected cards: many flush/straight draws → equities are close, made hands must protect.
spotq.explainTexturePaired: Paired board: watch out for trips/full houses, and "two pair" leaning on the board pair is really just one pair.
spotq.explainTextureSemi: Intermediate texture: one connector or two of a suit → a few draws, without being soaked.
spotq.whoCaller: the opponent is the raiser, YOU called
spotq.rangeSideHigh: A {{card}}-high board sticks to the RAISER's range (big Aces/Kings, broadways, big pairs).
spotq.rangeSideLow: A low board (9-high or less) sticks to the CALLER's range (connectors, small pairs, suited) — the raiser mostly has missed high cards.
spotq.rangeSideMid: A T/J-high board with no Ace or King is contested: both ranges hit it about equally → neutral.
spotq.rangeConclHero: → the advantage goes to YOUR range: you can bet/attack more often.
spotq.rangeConclVillain: → the advantage goes to THEIR range: careful, defend without overplaying.
spotq.rangeConclNeutral: → shared advantage: nobody dominates, play tight.
spotq.equityExplain: Your real equity ≈ {{eq}} (simulation vs their range). {{detail}}
spotq.equityValue: Strong made hand → you dominate a big part of their range.
spotq.equityDraw: You're behind now, your equity comes from your draw (you hit ~1 in 3).
spotq.equityBluffcatch: A medium pair: you beat their bluffs/misses but lose vs their made hands → medium equity.
spotq.equityAir: Almost nothing made: your equity mostly comes from overcards / a bit of showdown value.
spotq.bucketExplainValue: {{made}}: you beat their CALLING range → this is VALUE, you bet/raise to get paid by worse. Key question: "do worse hands pay me?" → yes.
spotq.bucketExplainBluffcatch: {{made}}: you beat their bluffs but lose to their value, and you don't improve → BLUFF-CATCH. You check/call, you DON'T bet (worse folds, better calls).
spotq.bucketExplainDraw: {{made}}: behind now, but a draw that can hit → DRAW. You play by the odds + implied odds, or as a semi-bluff.
spotq.bucketExplainAir: {{made}}: no useful pair nor draw → AIR. Either you give up, or you bluff if the board and opponent allow it.
spotq.storyDefend: {{opener}} opens in {{pos}}, you call in the BB{{multiway}}.

decision.title: The right move
decision.intro: Facing the opponent's action (their bet size), pick the right move: fold / call / raise / bet / check. The coach then reveals their range, your two golden questions, and the sizing tell.
decision.promptBet: The opponent BETS {{bb}}bb (~{{pct}}% of the pot). Your turn.
decision.promptCheck: The opponent CHECKS. It's on you (nobody bet).

decq.lessonFold: Not enough equity ({{eq}}) or dominated hand → fold.
decq.lessonCallBc: Bluff-catch: you beat their bluffs, you lose vs their value, and you don't improve → you CALL, you don't raise.
decq.lessonCallDraw: Draw: the odds + implied odds make the call profitable.
decq.lessonCall: Your equity ({{eq}}) beats the odds → call.
decq.lessonRaise: You dominate their calling range → raise to get paid by WORSE.
decq.lessonBetBluff: No showdown value, but the board/passivity let you represent the nuts → bluff (better hands fold).
decq.lessonBetValue: Strong hand: worse hands pay → you bet for value{{prot}}.
decq.lessonCheck: Neither clear value (worse won't pay) nor profitable bluff → check (control / showdown).
decq.twoFold: You beat neither their value nor enough of their bluffs to call → you give up.
decq.twoCallBc: "Do worse hands pay?" No → you don't bet/raise. But you beat their bluffs → you call.
decq.twoRaise: "Do worse hands pay?" YES → you raise for value.
decq.twoBetBluff: "Do better hands fold?" YES → profitable bluff.
decq.twoBetValue: "Do worse hands pay?" YES → value bet.
decq.twoCheck: "Do worse hands pay?" No. "Do better hands fold?" No. → check.
decq.sizeSmall: Sizing tell: they bet SMALL (~{{pct}}% pot) → their range leans weak (capped) → you can continue/attack wider.
decq.sizeBig: Sizing tell: big bet (~pot+) → polarized range (very strong OR bluff) → careful, it's rarely medium.
decq.sizeMid: Sizing tell: medium bet (~{{pct}}% pot) → balanced range.

tour.title: Tournament Training — MTT
tour.subtitle: Configure your multi-table tournament, then launch it
tour.antesNoteOn: Ante = 1 BB (paid by the big blind) from level 3.
tour.noHelpOffDesc: Coach, ranges and assists available. The hero has no timer (take your time).
tour.noHelpOnDesc: No help: coach, opponent ranges, follow-coach, angry coach and turbo all off. The hero plays on the clock (auto check/fold when it runs out).
tour.structureNote: Each level lasts {{min}} min (timer); the speed sets the size of each jump (×{{mult}}). Starting stack: {{bb}} BB.
tour.footerNote: You start at a {{n}}-max table; the rest of the field ({{rest}} players) plays in parallel.

train.clickSeat: Click a seat to take it

train.orClickTable: Or click directly on the table on the left

train.recoCoach: Coach active: it analyses every hand
train.descAmateur: Recreational: calls too much, passive, rarely bluffs

game.rotateBody: The table plays in landscape. Turn your phone sideways to continue.
game.angryCoachTip: Angry-coach mode: every decision against the coach triggers a siren and the coach gets angrier (levels 1→3). Three good decisions in a row calm him down.

coach.title: Coach Assistant
coach.hoverHint: hover — leave the profile to close
coach.dominatedFlush: ⚠︎ dominated flush: it's still an out (counted in your equity), but you can hit and still lose to a higher flush.
coach.qEquityCalc: How do you compute my equity?
coach.qPotoddsCalc: How do you compute the odds?
coach.qBluff: What if I try to bluff?
coach.faceRaiseTitle: Plan if the opponent bets / raises you, by size
coach.faceRaiseDisabled: Available when the advice is CHECK, BET or CALL
coach.footerPreflop: Range adapted to the number of players in the hand + equity estimated by simulation. Training advice, not a solver.
coach.footerPostflop: Equity estimated by simulation (Monte-Carlo) vs random hands. Heuristic training advice, not a solver.

hand.madeBoardOnly: High card: {{hi}} (+ the board pair, shared)

cadv.eqVsCapped: — the opponent bet then checked: their range is capped/weak
cadv.potOdds: Pot odds: you need {{odds}} equity to call {{toCall}}.
cadv.capped: CAPPED range: the opponent checked an earlier street — they'd have bet their strong hands before. Their delayed bet is therefore rarely the nuts (more bluffs / medium hands) → you can bluff-catch wider.
cadv.donkLead: Opponent LEAD: it's NOT the preflop raiser betting — a player who flats then LEADS/barrels (often out of position in the field) rarely does it with air. Their range is value-heavy (top pair+/overpair/sets) → your marginal pair is dominated more often, be careful (fold sooner than a classic bluff-catch).
cadv.facingRaise: You're facing a RAISE: it's the STRONGEST signal in poker. People almost never raise a plain top pair — their range is concentrated on two pair / sets / nuts (+ a few draws). Your pair (even top pair) is often BEHIND → don't stack off one pair facing a raise, fold unless you have a truly strong hand or the odds for a draw.
cadv.handBoard: Your hand: {{what}} — nothing of your own{{draws}}.
cadv.handLeanTwoPair: Your hand: top pair ({{pair}}) — your "two pair" leans on the board's pair (shared by everyone), play it as ONE pair.
cadv.oop: You're out of position: be more careful.
cadv.highCardValue: Your "high card" isn't nothing: your equity comes from your overcards (you can pair up), backup draws, and you beat their missed hands/bluffs at showdown.
cadv.sprCommitted: committed — with a strong hand, go all-in.
cadv.sprLow: fairly low, ready to invest big with a strong hand.
cadv.beatenMultiway: ⚠️ Multiway, and several opponents have CALLED multiple streets: calling that much multiway = STRENGTH (top pair+/two pair/sets), not air. Your pair ({{eq}}) is often BEHIND at least one of them → control the pot, don't overvalue / don't stack off one pair.
cadv.valueRaise: Strong hand: you dominate a good part of their range → raise for value.
cadv.bluffCatchBoard: ⚠️ A {{tex}} is possible that you DON'T have: your {{name}} is a BLUFF-CATCHER here. Raising would chase away their bluffs (which you beat) and only get called by their {{tex2}}/hands that beat you → you CALL (you beat their bluffs), you don't raise.
cadv.strongValueCall: Value hand, but raising would mainly get you called by better: call.
cadv.drawCallImplied: Draw: {{eq}} equity + implied odds (you win more when you hit) → profitable call.
cadv.vulnDraw: ⚠️ Vulnerable draw (low flush or paired board): reverse implied odds — you can hit and still lose, play carefully.
cadv.bluffcatch: Bluff-catcher: your equity ({{eq}}) beats the odds ({{odds}}) → you call to beat their bluffs, but don't raise.
cadv.callProfit: Your equity ({{eq}}) beats the pot odds ({{odds}}) → profitable call, even without a big hand.
cadv.foldVulnDraw: Vulnerable draw too expensive: {{eq}} equity, but reverse implied odds (low flush / paired board, facing aggression) → you sometimes hit only to lose. You'd need {{req}}. Fold.
cadv.foldDraw: Draw too expensive: {{eq}} equity vs {{odds}} required{{turn}}. Fold.
cadv.oneCardLeft: — and only one card to come (little implied value)
cadv.foldMarginal: You just barely have the raw odds ({{eq}} vs {{odds}}), BUT {{dom}}{{multi}}{{aggr}}{{oop}}: you need a safety margin (~{{req}}) against reverse implied odds → marginal fold.
cadv.oopSuffix: and you don't close the action (out of position)
cadv.foldNoOdds: Equity ({{eq}}) below the odds ({{odds}}) and no draw: folding is most profitable.
cadv.protectionBet: PROTECTION / equity-denial bet: against a capped range you're the most likely hand AHEAD, but it's VULNERABLE (under-pairs → set, overcards → pair). You bet to END the hand and deny a free outdraw — not to get paid.
cadv.protectVsDraw: PROTECTION bet: DRAW-heavy board (flush/straight possible) and you're very likely AHEAD. Bet ~2/3 pot to charge the draws and deny them a free card — checking your strong hand here lets the flush/straight complete for free (that's exactly how you get outdrawn).
cadv.thinValue: Opponent range CAPPED (repeated checks): your pair beats most of their hands → THIN value. Bet SMALL (~1/3 pot) to get paid by worse pairs — a big bet would fold them out.
cadv.valueBet: Strong hand: bet for value and make the draws pay.
cadv.overbet: Nut advantage on a high-card board: polarize and OVERBET — their inelastic range (top pairs) can't fold, so you max value.
cadv.exploitStation: EXPLOIT — loose/station opponent: they pay off, so size your value UP (and don't waste chips bluffing, they won't fold).
cadv.semiBluff: Strong draw: a semi-bluff wins the pot right away or by hitting.
cadv.onePairCheck: Medium pair: check to control the pot size and bluff-catch later.
cadv.flushBoardPotControl: {{n}} cards of one suit on the board: your {{name}} is BEHIND every made flush. Betting for value only gets called by flushes and folds out worse → check (pot control); you still beat the non-flushes and can fill up.
cadv.bluffNoSdv: You have no showdown value ({{eq}}) → checking almost always gives up the pot.
cadv.bluffRepNuts: The opponent bet then checked (capped range) and the {{tex}} board favors YOUR in-position calling range: you rep the nuts → big fold equity. This is a bluff, not over-aggression.
cadv.checkSpeculative: Hand with no showdown value, but a bluff here would be speculative (board not credible, opponent not capped or out of position) → check.
cadv.checkWeak: Weak hand: check (a bluff is only profitable on the right boards and opponents).
cadv.fpWhySemi: small sizing + your draw → fold equity + outs if called.

equity.potLine: The pot is <w>{{pot}}</w> and I must call <w>{{toCall}}</w>. My odds = bet ÷ (pot + bet) = {{toCall}} ÷ ({{pot}} + {{toCall}}) ≈ <gold>{{req}}</gold>: I need at least <gold>{{req}}</gold> equity to call.
equity.outsIntro_one: I have <w>{{count}}</w> out that improves me:
equity.outsIntro_other: I have <w>{{count}}</w> outs that improve me:
equity.outsRule: Outs rule ({{rule}}): these {{count}} out{{s}} ≈ <green>{{approx}}</green> draw equity{{cap}}{{weak}}.
equity.ruleFlop: ×4 on the flop, 2 cards to come
equity.ruleTurn: ×2 on the turn, 1 card to come
equity.noOuts: No card really improves me — my equity comes only from what I already beat.
equity.river: No more cards to come: my equity is only what I beat at showdown.
equity.realEquityOuts: My real equity ≈ <green>{{eq}}</green> (simulation: I replay the hand thousands of times and count the share I win).
equity.realEquityPreflop: My real equity ≈ <green>{{eq}}</green> — estimated by simulation: I replay this hand thousands of times dealing random opponent hands + board, and count the share I win ({{eq}} here). No outs to show before the flop.
equity.realEquityRiver: My real equity ≈ <green>{{eq}}</green> — estimated by simulation: I replay the showdowns thousands of times and count the share I win.
equity.whyBody: The {{approx}} only counts the times I hit an out. But I ALSO win without improving: <green>{{approx}}</green> (I improve) + <green>~{{sdv}}</green> (already ahead at showdown) ≈ <green>{{eq}}</green>.
equity.whyCalc: And that ~{{sdv}} is computed: I miss my outs ~<w2>{{noImp}}</w2> of the time × my hand already beats ~<w2>{{beat}}</w2> of their range at showdown (their bluffs, missed draws, lower cards) ≈ {{noImp}} × {{beat}} = ~{{sdv}}.
equity.vFold: ❌ I need {{req}} and only have {{eq}} → I do NOT have the odds → I fold.
equity.vFoldRealize: ⚠️ I do have the raw price ({{eq}} ≥ {{req}}) BUT I realize this equity poorly (value-heavy range, out of position) → calling isn't profitable, I fold.
equity.vCall: ✅ {{eq}} equity ≥ {{req}} required → I have the odds → I call.
equity.vImplied: ≈ I'm a bit below the direct odds ({{eq}} vs {{req}}), but my implied odds — I win big when I hit — make up for it → call.
equity.vRaiseValue: ✅ I have the odds ({{eq}} vs {{req}}) — and even better: I don't just call, I raise for value.
equity.vRaiseBluff: On odds alone I'm short ({{eq}} vs {{req}}), but I play this as a (semi-)bluff: the decision comes from fold equity, not the price.

padv.jamOnly: Facing {{n}} all-in(s), you can only CALL or FOLD — no flatting{{multi}}.
padv.jamMulti: , and the call-off range tightens a lot against several all-ins
padv.jamEquity: Equity decision: {{hero}} has ≈ {{eq}} equity, you need {{odds}} to call → {{verdict}}
padv.depthDeep: deep stacks → only premium hands call
padv.rangePos: By your position's range ({{position}} — {{situation}}), {{hero}} plays as: {{action}}.
padv.posIP: You're in position: you can play a bit wider.
padv.posOOP: You're out of position: tighten slightly.
padv.reshove: ~{{bb}} BB stack facing an open → RE-SHOVE (3-bet ALL-IN), not a small 3-bet: at this depth a small 3-bet COMMITS you (you can't fold to a 4-bet jam) and flatting OOP plays a low-SPR pot. You shove for fold equity + the opener's dead money.
padv.pushFold: Short stack (~{{bb}} BB) → PUSH/FOLD: you jam or you fold, no more postflop play.
padv.shortStack: Short stack (~{{bb}} BB): favor 3-bet ALL-IN (re-shove) / fold, few flats.
padv.deepStack: Deep stack (~{{bb}} BB): suited/connected hands gain value.
padv.bigOpen: Big open (~{{bb}} BB): no fold equity → cut the 3-bet bluffs, value only.
padv.smallOpen: Small open (~{{bb}} BB): full polarized 3-bet range + wide flats.
padv.small3bet: Small 3-bet (~{{r}}× the open): good odds → you continue much wider.
padv.big3bet: Big 3-bet (~{{r}}× the open): tighten a lot (especially flats).
padv.multiway: Several players already in the hand: tighten (especially offsuit hands).
padv.bluffPolar: Hand played as a polarized BLUFF / semi-bluff: its value comes from fold equity + blockers + playability when called — NOT showdown equity. Don't trust the raw % shown here.
padv.setMine: Set-mine: you call cheaply to flop a set (~1 in 8) and stack a deep stack (~{{bb}} BB) — implied odds make the call profitable even if raw equity looks low.
padv.suitedDeep: Suited hand played IP deep: you call for playability + implied odds (flush/straight), not just the direct price.
padv.closing: You CLOSE the action (big blind): {{eq}} equity for a price of only {{odds}} → you have the price to call, defend wide at this rate (K-high, connectors, pairs…) since no one can raise behind.
padv.icm: ICM pressure (~{{n}}%): near the bubble / a pay jump, busting is costly. Tighten your jams and call-offs — surviving is worth more than booking a small edge.

cask.equity: You have about {{eq}} chance to win the hand. {{tail}}
cask.equityOdds: To call, you need at least {{odds}}: {{verdict}}
cask.equityFree: No one bet: you can check for free or bet for value.
cask.oddsImplied: you're slightly below the direct odds, BUT your draw gives implied odds (you win more when you hit) → the call is still fine.
cask.oddsUnder: you're below → on the direct odds, calling loses long-term (hence the fold).
cask.raiseValue: With {{eq}} equity, raising for value is excellent: you make weaker hands and draws pay.
cask.raiseSemi: Raising as a semi-bluff is playable: even behind now, your {{draws}} can win you a big pot.
cask.raiseRisky: Raising here is risky: with only {{eq}} equity, you mainly get called by better.
cask.equityCalc: I compute it by simulation (Monte-Carlo): I replay this hand ~1000–1500 times dealing the missing board cards and the {{n}} opponent(s) hands at random, then compare your hand to theirs each time. The % of times you win (ties count as a fraction) = your equity, ~{{eq}} here. The more simulations, the more precise the figure.
cask.potoddsNone: No one bet: you have nothing to call, so no pot odds to meet.
cask.potoddsCalc: It's pure math: you must call {{toCall}} to try to win the pot. Required equity = amount to call ÷ (total pot after your call) = {{toCall}} ÷ ({{pot}} + {{toCall}}) ≈ {{odds}}. So you must win the hand at least {{odds}} of the time for the call to be profitable. You win ~{{eq}} → {{verdict}}
cask.faceNone: Available when the advice is CHECK, BET or CALL.
cask.bluff: To bluff, {{verb}} to make the opponent give up. A size of about ⅔ pot (~{{size}}) is credible: you risk {{size}} to win {{pot}}, so they must fold at least ~{{fe}} of the time (bet ÷ (pot + bet)) for the bluff to profit. ⚠️ Risk: if they call, you have only ~{{eq}} equity left — you lose your bet most of the time. {{tail}}
cask.bluffSemi: Good news: with your {{draws}}, it's a SEMI-bluff — even when called you can still hit, so far less risky.
cask.bluffPure: Without a draw, it'd be a "pure" bluff: you rely only on their fold. Reserve it for boards that hit your range and opponents capable of folding.

rng.openAggrKeep: Chart strength {{sv}} ≥ open threshold {{th}} → this hand is part of the opening range → kept.
rng.openAggrCut: Chart strength {{sv}} < open threshold {{th}} → it never opens here → removed by the OPEN.
rng.openCheckKeep: Too weak to open ({{sv}} < {{th}}) → it just checks the big blind → kept.
rng.openCheckCut: Strong enough to open ({{sv}} ≥ {{th}}) → it would have BET, not checked → excluded.
rng.openLimpKeep: Limped hand (passive style, rare) → kept as a fraction.
rng.p1AggrBluff: Light / polarized 3-bet (bluff) → kept as a small fraction.
rng.p1AggrCut: Doesn't 3-bet facing an open → excluded from the RE-RAISE.
rng.p1CallKeep: In the flatting band (strong enough to defend, not enough to 3-bet) → calls the open → kept.
rng.p1CallPremiumCut: Premium: it would have 3-bet, not just called → nearly excluded.
rng.p1CallWeakCut: Too weak to defend against the open → excluded by the CALL.
rng.p2CallKeep: Core value range that calls a 3-bet (88+, AK, suited broadways…) → kept.
rng.p2CallPremiumCut: Premium: it 4-bets → excluded from a plain CALL.
rng.p3AggrNuts: The nuts (AA/KK) → jam over the 4-bet → kept.
rng.pfCheckStrong: Strong hand ({{s}}): on this board it BETS ~{{bet}} of the time → a CHECK only keeps it at ~{{keep}} → heavily reduced (that's what a capped range is).
rng.pfCheckDraw: Draw: it semi-bluffs ~{{semi}} → a CHECK keeps it at ~{{keep}}.
rng.pfCheckWeak: Weak/medium hand ({{s}}): it checks most often (~{{keep}}) → kept.
rng.pfBetDraw: Draw: semi-bluff ~{{semi}} → kept as a fraction.
rng.pfBetWeak: Weak hand: almost never bets (bluff ~{{bluff}}) → nearly excluded from the BET.
rng.pfRaiseStrongKeep: Very strong ({{s}} ≥ raise threshold) → raises → kept.
rng.pfRaiseStrongCut: Very strong: it would have RAISED → a plain {{act}} reduces it heavily.
rng.pfCallOdds: Enough equity ({{s}}) vs the odds ({{odds}}) → calls → kept.
rng.pfCallDraw: Draw with the (implied) odds → calls → kept.
rng.pfCallLight: Called as a fraction (light call) → partly kept.
rng.pfCallCut: Not enough odds ({{s}} < {{odds}}) and nothing to hit → doesn't call → excluded from the CALL.
rng.pfRaiseBluffKeep: Raises as a (semi-)bluff / thin value → kept as a fraction.
rng.pfRaiseBluffCut: Doesn't raise here → excluded from the RAISE.
rng.effPolarTight: polarized + tightened range (multiway squeeze → value-heavy, few bluffs)
rng.effCondensed: condensed / capped range (median — rarely the nuts)
rng.effCapped: capped range (big hands would often have bet)

story.title: In a pro's head
story.readChecked: it's checked to me → I take over
story.readCapped: he bet then checked → his range is capped, he rarely has the nuts
story.readDonk: he leads out (donk) → a fairly value-defined range
story.readStrong: he's barreling hard → his range leans value
story.pHand: Me, I've got {{hand}}{{draws}}. My equity is around {{eq}}%{{outs}}{{odds}}.
story.lSemiBluff: So I'm thinking semi-bluff: against a weak range with a strong draw, I have TWO ways to win — fold him out now, or hit later.
story.lValue: I have a real value hand — I need to make it pay, not hide it.
story.lBluffCatch: My hand only beats his bluffs, but my equity ({{eq}}%) beats the price ({{odds}}) → a clean bluff-catch.
story.lCall: My equity ({{eq}}%) beats the price ({{odds}}), so continuing is profitable.
story.lCheck: Not enough to value-bet, nothing worth protecting that would pay → I control the pot.
story.lFold: My equity ({{eq}}%) is below the price ({{odds}}), and his range beats me too often → no reason to pay.
story.lFoldRealize: I do have the raw price ({{eq}}% ≥ {{odds}}), but I realize this equity poorly (out of position, a range that dominates me) → calling isn't profitable once realization is factored in.
story.lBetThin: I can squeeze a bit of thin value here.
story.pSpr: The SPR is low (~{{spr}}), so I'm ready to commit big.
story.goalFold: not paying off a range that has me dominated
story.goalCheck: keeping the pot controlled and seeing a free card
story.goalCall: paying because the price is right
story.goalSemiBluff: folding him out now OR hitting my draw
story.exploitStation: And above all: this is a loose player who pays off everything — so I size my value UP instead of hiding it, and I don't waste time bluffing, he won't fold.

pstory.setRfi: It's folded to me in the {{pos}}.
pstory.setVsopen: A {{size}}bb open in front of me, the decision's on me in the {{pos}}.
pstory.setSqueeze: An open + cold-callers ahead of me in the {{pos}} — a squeeze spot.
pstory.setVs3bet: I opened and got 3-bet. I'm in the {{pos}}.
pstory.setVs3betCold: An open then a 3-bet in front of me — I have to react cold in the {{pos}}.
pstory.setVs4betCold: Open, 3-bet and 4-bet in front of me — a scorching cold spot in the {{pos}}.
pstory.setJam: Short stack (~{{bb}}bb) in the {{pos}}: jam-or-fold mode.
pstory.decOpen: {{key}} is in my opening range here → I take the initiative.
pstory.decIso: {{key}} is strong enough to isolate the limpers and play the pot in the lead.
pstory.dec3betValue: {{key}} 3-bets for value — I build the pot with a hand that dominates his range.
pstory.dec3betBluff: {{key}} as a light 3-bet: I polarize, fold him out or play a pot with the initiative.
pstory.decCall: {{key}} just calls — I see a cheap flop{{ip}}.
pstory.dec4bet: {{key}}: I 4-bet for value, I don't hand him the initiative.
pstory.decJam: {{key}}: at this depth it's jam-or-fold — I shove, fold equity + my equity do the rest.
pstory.decCallJam: {{key}}: I call the jam — my equity ({{eq}}%) beats the price.
pstory.decFold: {{key}} is out of my range here → too dominated to enter.
pstory.decSetMine: {{key}} calls to set-mine: the dead money and the multiway pot give the implied odds — I flop a set ~1 in 8 and get paid by the bloated pot.
pstory.decFoldPair: {{key}} would love to set-mine, but the implied odds are too thin here (heads-up, a short 3-bettor, or ICM pressure) → I pass rather than call blind.
pstory.ipNote: and I realize well in position
pstory.antiVs4bet: And if he 4-bets me? I already know: {{cont}}.
pstory.contJam: I jam (strong enough to stack off)
pstory.contFold: I let it go (my bluff will have done its job)
pstory.goalOpen: take the initiative and the fold equity
pstory.goal3bet: isolate and play a dominating pot
pstory.goalCall: see a cheap flop with upside
pstory.goalCallJam: collect because the price is right
pstory.goalJam: maximize my fold equity while I'm committed
pstory.goalFold: save my chips for a better spot

plan.title: Pro Plan
plan.lockedCta: Go Pro for the full line
plan.turnGiveUp: No equity → give up, no barrel.
plan.turnBluffcatch: Bluff-catch: pay once, fold a second barrel unless you improve.
plan.turnThinValue: Thin value: bet once then check (one-and-done).
plan.turnSemibluff: Semi-bluff: barrel good cards, give up on bricks.
plan.turnReeval: Re-evaluate the turn by the card and their reaction.
plan.turnRaiseTurn: Keep going for value, raise the turn.
plan.turnTrapBet: Trapping: check to bet/raise the turn for value.
plan.turnPotControl: Pot control: take a free card, aim for showdown, call a reasonable turn.
plan.turnFreeCardDraw: Free card for your draw: continue if you hit.
plan.glossCheckBack: You're in position (last to act): checking closes the action → you take a free card and see the turn.
plan.glossCheckFold: Check, and give up to any bet (no equity).
plan.glossCheckCall: Check to call a reasonable bet (pot control / bluff-catch).
plan.glossCheckRaise: Check then raise their bet (value trap or semi-bluff).
plan.glossBetFold: Bet for value, but fold if raised (you only beat bluffs).
plan.glossBetCall: Bet for value and call a raise (your hand is strong enough).
plan.glossBet3bet: Bet then re-raise / jam if raised (big value).

perf.title: Session review
perf.verdictS: Crusher level — almost perfectly aligned with the coach. 🔥
perf.verdictA: Very solid: your decisions track the coach, few leaks.
perf.verdictB: Good, but leaks to fix to reach the next level.
perf.verdictC: Regular gaps with the coach — work to do.
perf.verdictD: Lots of gaps: rebuild the basics street by street.

crit.lMicroJamFold: At {{bb}}bb you're in jam-or-fold: the blinds dwarf your stack, so you shove almost any two (this is NOT about hand strength — {{key}} is still trash). Folding here is very slightly -EV, not a real leak.
crit.lGoodPre: Your choice matches the standard range for this position/situation. ✅
crit.lOpenBorderline: {{key}} is just outside the standard range ({{rec}}) — a marginal open that defends/mixes here, not a leak.
crit.lTooWide: {{key}} isn't in your range here — playing this hand loses money long-term.
crit.lTooPassivePre: {{key}} should {{rec}} (value + initiative), not just call — you leave value and fold equity behind.
crit.lFoldTooTight: {{key}} is strong enough to {{rec}} — folding it is a leak.
crit.lOverAggroPre: {{key}} is more of a call here; raising makes you play a big pot dominated part of the time.
crit.lFoldSlightlyTight: {{key}} could call (odds/playability), but the fold is still defensible.
crit.lGoodPost: Your decision matches the recommended move. ✅
crit.lSizeOverbet: ⚠️ Sizing: you bet {{pct}}% of the pot{{allin}} — an OVERBET with a value hand. You chase away the worse hands (2nd pair, weak ace, draws) that would call ~⅔ pot → you lose value.{{tail}}
crit.lSizeOverbetLowSpr: That said, at low SPR (~{{spr}}) you get it all-in anyway: the mistake stays minor.
crit.lSizeOverbetHighSpr: Aim for ~⅔–¾ pot to keep their range wider.
crit.lSizeTooSmall: ⚠️ Sizing: {{pct}}% of the pot is too little for a strong hand — you leave value and protect poorly against draws. Aim for ~⅔ pot.
crit.lContinueTooMuch: Your equity ({{eq}}) is below the odds ({{odds}}): calling/raising loses long-term.
crit.lSlowplayOK: Checking a locked monster ({{made}}) multiway is a valid trap: your hand has nothing to protect (paired board), and betting would fold out the air that can bluff/jam behind. The coach "value-bets" by default, but here the check (trap) is at least as profitable. ✅
crit.lTooPassivePost: You had a hand/equity to bet or raise (value + protection) — checking/calling leaves value behind.
crit.lFoldTooMany: You fold a hand that should have bet for value.
crit.lOverAggroPost: Raising turns a showdown/bluff-catch hand into a target: you fold out the worse hands and get paid by the better ones.
crit.lMissedFold: You had the odds ({{eq}} ≥ {{odds}}): folding throws away EV.

rev.sizeBig: · big bet → POLARIZED range (nuts or bluffs, few medium hands)
rev.sizeSmall: · small bet → CAPPED / wide range (thin value included)
rev.vPale: PALE GOLD — still in, but reduced (played as a mix)
rev.startLine: Start: {{combos}} combos{{blocked}} · estimated survival ≈ {{pct}}% of their combos
rev.blocked: (reduced — you block one of their cards)
rev.emptyMsg: No action from this player yet: full starting range. Advance the film ▶ to see each action's effect on {{key}}.
rev.replay: Replay the film from the start

sess.replayStart: Replay the hand from the start
sess.turboTip: TURBO — auto-folds and only skips hands where the coach folds you; stops on everything else (check included). Click → Normal
sess.fastTip: Fast — instant bots, stops at your decision. Click → Turbo
sess.accelTip: Speed up — instant bots. Click → Fast
sess.replayHint: · ↻ auto-replay (0.5s/step, loops) · ←/→ navigate · Space judge · Esc close
sess.reviveTip: Re-create this exact spot as a playable sandbox — opponents keep their range, their cards are drawn at random within it

spos.headerSub: Configure every detail for a tailored session
spos.streetHint: Fill the board up to the chosen street to start there (flop = 3 cards, turn = 4, river = 5). Preflop = no cards.
spos.oppHint: Leave a bot's cards empty = random draw. Fill them = forced hand to simulate a precise spot.
spos.manualDesc: Uncheck "Play live" for manual mode: in the room, click the player whose turn it is to enter their action (fold / check / call / bet). The coach updates on every bet.
spos.manualTip: Manual mode: you drive every bet by clicking in the room
spos.valBoard: Fill the {{need}} board cards to start at the {{street}}.

xtra.sitOutMsg: You're away — click Sit In to return
xtra.hintPinned: 📌 pinned — ✕ or Esc to close · click a cell for the WHY
xtra.hintUnpinned: ⏸ click or Space to pin · click a cell for the WHY

simw.title: Watch the coach play
simw.desc: The coach takes on your bots over a full tournament. You see the result + every hand where there was action, replayable with its analysis (equity, range, reasoning).
simw.running: The coach is playing the tournament…
simw.handsSub: {{n}} action hands (folds excluded) — click to replay with the coach's analysis
simw.noHands: No action hands in this tournament — run another one.
